clinical_history: ECG from an elderly woman with recurrent palpitations. The rhythm is most consistent with which ONE of the following mechanisms?
diagnosis: The ECG shows a narrow (normal QRS) complex, very regular tachycardia (paroxysmal supraventricular ventricular tachycardia/PSVT) at about 150/min. Probable small pseudo-S waves are evident in lead II and pseudo-R waves in aVR and V1, consistent with AVNRT as specific mechanism, with these retrograde (negative) P waves coming just after the QRS. Nonspecific ST-T changes are present, along with an early precordial R wave transition.
